title: Aberdeen Group Assessment of Unicenter and Jasmine for Computer Associates
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: Letter-form report from Peter S. Kastner (Group Vice President, Aberdeen Group) to David M. Hood (Director of Analyst Relations, Computer Associates) following a two-day analyst roundtable evaluation of CA's Unicenter and Jasmine products. Kastner delivers pointed positioning recommendations to maximize 1997 revenue, including brand-led advertising, ISV instrumentation, a VAR program, non-traditional environments, a national fault-response center, and competitive collateral against Tivoli. The letter also proposes a broad Aberdeen-CA consulting engagement.
importance_rationale: First-person Aberdeen-to-CA strategy letter capturing a pivotal 1996 moment for Unicenter and Jasmine when CA still held dominant share in enterprise systems management and Tivoli was ascendant under new IBM ownership.
relevance_rationale: Framework (branding, ISV instrumentation, partner channels, cost-of-ownership) remains applicable to enterprise management vendors; specific product references dated.
prescience_rationale: Strong prediction that Tivoli's 1996 product set would not be IBM's product three years later is partially verified — IBM Tivoli was repeatedly rearchitected and eventually subsumed into broader IBM portfolios.
